reasoning: The most recent data points for US diesel prices from September to mid-October 2025 show a strong downward momentum (falling from 3.766 on Sept 8 to 3.620 on Oct 20). Given this short-term trend, the probability of an increase by November 2 is significantly below 0.5. Over the medium term (late November), short-term momentum effects decay, bringing the probability closer to the base rate. For late January, winter seasonal factors—which historically can drive middle distillate prices higher due to heating demand—partially offset the current downward momentum, though long-horizon uncertainty pushes the estimate strongly toward 0.5. Thus, the estimates progressively approach 0.5 for more distant targets.
